title: PDF Processing Guide
description: Extract, merge, split, OCR, watermark, author and transform PDFs. One toolkit reads a messy PDF into clean structured data, modifies a document, or generates a brand-new PDF from scratch.

cta.label: Fork and run
cta.note: Runs on Jetty's managed sandbox. No setup. Free for your first 10 runs.

example_outputs.title: Research-paper tables → JSON
example_outputs.summary: Extract every table from a 20-page PLOS Medicine article into one strictly-valid JSON file (empty cells null, word spacing recovered).

input.label: Evaluation of the sugar-sweetened beverage tax in Oakland, United States, 2015–2019
input.citation: White JS, Basu S, Kaplan S, Madsen KA, Villas-Boas SB, Schillinger D (2023). PLOS Medicine 20(4): e1004212.

outputs.description: Every table as a strict-JSON array (source_page, table_index, headers, rows, row_count).
The same tables as an Excel workbook.

excerpt.label: Page 9 — adjusted difference-in-differences model (1 of 5 tables)

example_outputs.title: Stamp a PDF CONFIDENTIAL
example_outputs.summary: Overlay a bold diagonal CONFIDENTIAL watermark on all 20 pages and emit a single watermarked PDF — and only that PDF.

input.label: Evaluation of the sugar-sweetened beverage tax in Oakland (same CC BY source PDF)

values.operation: watermark — stamp every page with a large diagonal semi-transparent CONFIDENTIAL watermark and save as output.pdf

outputs.description: All 20 pages stamped with a diagonal red CONFIDENTIAL watermark.

example_outputs.title: Author a one-page summary PDF
example_outputs.summary: Generate a clean, branded one-page Document Summary PDF from scratch — source title, page count and table count — proving the toolkit authors PDFs, not just consumes them.

input.label: Source document the summary describes (same CC BY source PDF)

values.operation: create a clean one-page summary PDF titled Document Summary listing the source PDF title, page count and number of tables; save as output.pdf

outputs.description: A newly authored one-page summary PDF (title, page count, table count).

inputs.label: Input PDF(s)
inputs.description: One or more PDF files. Uploaded files land in /app/assets/.

inputs.label: Operation
inputs.description: Pick an operation, or describe it in natural language (e.g. "extract the tables into JSON", "stamp every page CONFIDENTIAL"). Honored as of runbook v1.2.0 — the runbook runs ONLY the chosen operation and emits only its deliverables.

inputs.label: Output filename
inputs.description: Name for the primary output PDF (structural / create operations).

metrics._note: Live counters — supplied by the Jetty backend at render time, not authored here.

demo_ideas.title: Government form → extracted fields
demo_ideas.why: Forms are the #1 real-world PDF pain. A US federal form (IRS W-9 / 1040, public domain) reads as instantly legitimate and shows field/label extraction without licensing risk.
demo_ideas.input_suggestion: IRS W-9 or 1040 blank form (public domain, irs.gov).

demo_ideas.title: Scanned document → searchable text (OCR)
demo_ideas.why: Exercises the tesseract/pdf2image path that text extraction can't touch. A scanned historical document is a visually obvious image-in / text-out payoff. Requires tesseract in the snapshot.
demo_ideas.input_suggestion: A public-domain scanned document (Library of Congress / archive.org / a US gov scan).

demo_ideas.title: Merge several PDFs into one
demo_ideas.why: The canonical 'combine documents' use case — e.g. assemble per-client invoices into one packet. Verifiable (page count = sum).
demo_ideas.input_suggestion: 2–3 small PDFs (can be generated with the create operation).

demo_ideas.title: Pull every figure out of a PDF
demo_ideas.why: High visual payoff (a gallery of figures) — BUT only works on PDFs with embedded raster images. The PLOS article's figures are vector, so it yields nothing; pick a PDF with real embedded images.
demo_ideas.input_suggestion: A report or slide-export PDF with embedded raster charts/photos.
